qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Template Injection in payment redirect URLs allows unauthenticated code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes Stored XSS (CWE-79) due to missing HTML escaping of Product Title, Variant Title, and Variant SKU fields on the Commerce Inventory page. This finding describes SSTI in PaymentsController::actionPay() via redirect/cancelUrl parameters passed to renderObjectTemplate(). These are different vulnerability classes (XSS vs SSTI), different components (Inventory page vs PaymentsController), different sinks (HTML output encoding vs Twig template rendering), and different root causes (missing HTML escaping in inventory template vs unsanitized template rendering in payment flow).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-cfpv-rmpf-f624, CVE-2026-29175) describes Stored XSS (CWE-79) in the Craft Commerce Inventory page where Product Title, Variant Title, and Variant SKU fields are rendered without HTML escaping, enabling session hijacking via cookie exfiltration. The patch (commit 9f0638a4fb29ed8295a463385a7cc49ec986e33a) would add HTML output encoding to the inventory page template. The sole qualifying finding 'ssti_in_payment_urls' describes SSTI in PaymentsController::actionPay() via redirect/cancelUrl parameters — a different vulnerability class (SSTI vs XSS), different component (PaymentsController vs Inventory page), and different sink (Twig renderObjectTemplate vs HTML output).
evidence: Advisory root cause: Product Title, Variant Title, Variant SKU fields rendered without HTML escaping on /admin/commerce/inventory (Stored XSS, CWE-79)
Finding ssti_in_payment_urls: SSTI via PaymentsController::actionPay() at src/controllers/PaymentsController.php:366-374 — different vulnerability class, different component, different root cause
